rawFiles.name: logistics-cluster-supply-routes-snapshot-middle-east-2026-05-25.html
rawFiles.notes: Fetched for checksum review only; raw HTML is not committed.

transforms: Review only the point-location observations in the corridors into Syria section.
Use the source publication/revision date, 2026-05-25, as both statusAsOf and sourceReferences[].sourceRecordDate.
Attach route-table status only where the named border crossing point or port maps to an existing canonical location ID.
Preserve concise notes that identify the source context as a corridor or sea-route observation.
Normalize high-level corridor rows into route snapshots with origin, destination, transit countries, matched location IDs, source names, and indicative lead time.
Hold route geometry, ambiguous alternate border crossing points, and regional rows that are not Syria route/corridor observations.
reviewNotes: Imported 5 dated point-location status snapshot records and 5 high-level route snapshot records from the 25 May 2026 regional supply-route snapshot. Point observations cover Nasib/Jaber, Cilvegozu-Bab alHawa, Al Masnaa/Jdidet, Latakia Port, and Tartous Port. Route observations cover the Gulf-Jordan-Syria corridor via Saudi Arabia, Turkiye/Caucasus to northwest Syria, Lebanon-Syria, Jordan-Syria, and Europe-Syria Mediterranean routes. Held the Iraq-Syria Al Waleed/Al Tanf or Rabiaa/Al-Yaroubiyah alternate row, duplicated broader regional rows, Red Sea multimodal constraints, Lebanon onward corridors, exact route geometry, and tactical/live routing details. No location operationalStatus values were changed.
